# Liber

> Liber helps homeschool families capture everyday moments of learning and turn them into confidence, records and evidence — without creating school at home.

Liber is a learning-journal web app for relaxed, interest-led, unschooling and neurodivergent homeschool families, made in Australia. Parents capture ordinary moments (a photo and a few lines); Liber helps them see the learning already in those moments and quietly builds a private record they can use for registration, reviews or an Authorised Person visit.

Liber is deliberately not a curriculum tool: no lesson plans, no worksheets, no grades, and it never fabricates learning that didn't happen. Parents' own words stay at the centre.

## Who it's for
- Relaxed, interest-led and unschooling families
- Newly-registering homeschool families preparing for reviews or home visits (e.g. NSW Authorised Person visits)
- Families with neurodivergent learners
- Homeschool families across Australia and New Zealand (and beyond)

## Key features
- Moment capture: photo plus a short note, in your own words
- Reflection: see the learning a moment already contains
- Patterns over time: threads and a fuller picture of how a child learns
- Private by design: family data is never shared or sold
- Liber+ (paid): full history, richer learning stories, review-ready exports, optional curriculum tagging (never required)

## Pricing
- Free: capture unlimited everyday moments and start your record
- Liber+: paid subscription (AUD) adding history, patterns, stories and exports

## Pages
- https://liber.net.au/ — what Liber is and how it works
- https://liber.net.au/guides/ — practical guides for homeschool evidence and records
- https://liber.net.au/guides/nesa-home-visit-evidence/ — what to show an Authorised Person at a NSW home visit
- https://liber.net.au/privacy/ — privacy policy
- https://liber.net.au/terms/ — terms of service
